steps.title: Identify the structure
steps.content:
Two independent clauses: The sand at Balos Beach in Greece is a more unusual shade, though | deposits of crushed coral and other organic matter lend the sand a unique pink hue.

Both have subjects and main verbs (is; lend), so we need a strong boundary between them.

steps.title: Apply the IC + IC rule
steps.content:
Two independent clauses can be joined by a semicolon. Though attaches to the first clause as an adverb (shade, though = "shade, however").

B uses just a comma (comma splice). C adds a comma after though, still a comma splice. D attaches though as a subordinator to the second clause — but that creates a fragment after the semicolon.

A (shade, though;) correctly uses a semicolon between the two ICs. A is correct.
